items.title: In The Mood For Love
items.subtitle: Wong Kar-wai • 2000
items.note: The cinematography of this film represents the peak of visual storytelling. Every frame is a painting, capturing the unspoken yearning and shared loneliness of two people in a way that feels both timeless and deeply intimate.

items.title: The Godfather Part II
items.subtitle: Francis Ford Coppola • 1974
items.note: Storytelling through eyes!!! Another level shit

items.title: Oppenheimer
items.subtitle: Christopher Nolan • 2023
items.note: Prometheus stole fire from the gods and gave it to man. For this he was chained to a rock and tortured for eternity...Nahhh seriously WTF!!

items.title: Breaking Bad
items.subtitle: Vince Gilligan • 2008
items.note: If you know death is comming for you, how would you life your remaining days? Serious phsyological transformation of a man when he chooses to live his remaining days on his own terms. Absolute cinema!!! My fav series BTW!

items.title: Better Call Saul
items.subtitle: Vince Gilligan • 2015
items.note: I'd confess to all my crimes and serve 87 years in prison if it meant you'd walk free.   But you're not nerdy enough to understand that.

items.title: The Sopranos
items.subtitle: David Chase • 1999
items.note: Imagine being a mob boss and going to therapy. This series helps me understand psychological depth on another level.

items.title: House of Cards
items.subtitle: Beau Willimon • 2013
items.note: For those of us climbing to the top of the food chain, There can be no mercy. There is but one rule, HUNT OR BE HUNTED!!!

items.title: Peaky Blinders
items.subtitle: Steven Knight • 2013
items.note: Ahhh the cinematography of this series is just something else!!!

items.title: Chernobyl
items.subtitle: Craig Mazin • 2019
items.note: A chilling and meticulously researched account of the 1986 disaster. It powerfully conveys the human cost of bureaucratic failure and the courage of those who faced the unthinkable.

items.title: The Queen's Gambit
items.subtitle: Scott Frank • 2020
items.note: It's a visually stunning and emotionally nuanced exploration of genius, addiction, and the search for belonging. Anya Taylor-Joy is just something else!!!

items.title: True Detective
items.subtitle: Nic Pizzolatto • 2014
items.note: I think human consciousness is a tragic misstep in evolution, We became too self aware

items.title: Friends
items.subtitle: David Crane • 1994
items.note: Perfectly captures the essence of friendship and the relatable struggles of young adulthood with humor, heart, and characters that feel like old friends.

items.title: The Office
items.subtitle: Greg Daniels • 2005
items.note: Laugh so hard my stomach hurts!
